medical_history.chief_complaint: decreased vision and floaters for five years
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: paresthesia and weakness in his hands and legs to the level of the knees, with reflexes absent for seven years. A moderately severe autonomic neuropathy (alternating painless diarrhea and constipation) was present. At later stage the patient developed syncope, which responded to orthostatic hypotension. Cardiac ultrasound revealed mild degenerative membrane changes.
medical_history.family_history: three-generation family with eleven members. The primary ocular signs were vitreous opacities, beginning from the third or fourth decade, accompanied by retinal vasculitis, hemorrhages, and widespread pinpoint deposits in the peripheral retina.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: visual acuity for patient III:1 was 20/1000 in both eyes. The ocular anterior segment and intraocular pressure were normal. Glass, wool-like vitreous opacities were observed in both eyes, which obscured the view to the posterior pole. Peripheral dot-blot hemorrhages were also observed. FFA showed small tufts of neovascularization, vascular fluorescein leakage and staining, and a possible non-perfusion area.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Serum VEGF levels were raised (408 pg/ml of III:2 and 453 pg/ml of IV:2) compared to controls (average 209.809 ± 93.833 pg/ml). Vitreous VEGF levels were also raised (15.94 pg/ml of III:2 and 17.17 pg/ml of IV:2) compared to controls (average 5.09 ± 4.45 pg/ml).
A pathogenic transversion in exon 2 (c.G106C, p.Ala36Pro) of the TTR gene was identified.
Vitreous samples showed staining with Congo Red and characteristic yellow-green birefringence indicative of amyloid deposits.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Case 1: Small tufts of neovascularization, vascular fluorescein leakage and staining, and a possible non-perfusion area. Case 2: Irregular background fluorescein, local fluorescein leakage, and widespread hyperfluorescent spots (window defect).
Case 2: Normal macula, irregularities within the retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) layer corresponding to pinpoint yellowish spots. Case 3: Normal macula.
Case 2: Vitreous opacities and membrane formation.

other_tests.findings: Case 3: ERG was within normal limits.
Case 3: EOG was within normal limits.
Case 2: Vitreous opacities removed, visual acuity improved from 20/1000 to 20/50. Case 3: Vitreous opacities removed, visual acuity improved from 20/1000 to 30/50.
